  "response": "{
  "judgement": "TP",
  "analysis": "This is a real stored XSS, although the report overstates one part of the write path. The sink is genuine: `select-organization.ftl` renders `organization.alias` inside an inline JavaScript string in `onclick`. The alias reaches that template through `OrganizationAuthenticator.shouldUserSelectOrganization()` -> `ProfileBean.getOrganizations()` -> `OrganizationBean.getAlias()` with no JavaScript-context escaping. Keycloak configures FreeMarker for HTML output, but that only gives HTML escaping; it does not apply `?js_string`-style escaping for a JavaScript string literal. That matters because HTML entities such as `'` are decoded by the browser before the inline event handler is executed, so a quote in the alias can still break out of the JS string. The create path is sufficient to store such a payload: `OrganizationsResource.create()` only calls `ReservedCharValidator.validateNoSpace()`, and that validator does not reject quotes or backticks. Even with `(`, `)`, `;`, `/`, and spaces blocked, code execution is still possible because allowed characters such as `'`, `` ` ``, and `-` are enough to build valid JavaScript in this handler context. The report is inaccurate about the update path, because aliases are effectively immutable after creation, but that does not invalidate the vulnerable create-to-render path. Reachability is conditional, not theoretical: the page is shown when a user belongs to multiple organizations and the organization scope resolves to `ANY`, and the victim is expected to click one of the listed organizations, which triggers the malicious handler. Default login-page CSP does not neutralize this, because Keycloak's default CSP does not define `script-src`, so inline event handlers are still allowed. The source requires realm-management privileges, so this is a privileged stored-XSS vector rather than a low-privilege one, but it is still a true CWE-79 issue.",
  "FP_Reason": [],
  "evidence": "Sink: `themes/src/main/resources/theme/base/login/select-organization.ftl:12` renders `onclick=\"document.forms[0]['kc.org'].value = '${organization.alias}'; document.forms[0].requestSubmit()\"`. Data flow: `services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/organization/authentication/authenticators/browser/OrganizationAuthenticator.java:246-262` puts `new ProfileBean(user, session)` into the template; `services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/email/freemarker/beans/ProfileBean.java:90-99` maps member organizations to `OrganizationBean`; `services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/forms/login/freemarker/model/OrganizationBean.java:38-43` copies `organization.getAlias()` directly. Source: `services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/organization/admin/resource/OrganizationsResource.java:99-113` accepts the alias and only applies `ReservedCharValidator.validateNoSpace()`. Validator weakness: `server-spi-private/src/main/java/org/keycloak/utils/ReservedCharValidator.java:35,59-62` blocks `[:/?#@!$&()*+,;=\\[\\]\\\\]` and whitespace, but not `'`, `\"`, or `` ` ``. Rendering context: `services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/theme/freemarker/DefaultFreeMarkerProvider.java:59-63` sets HTML output format only, with no JavaScript-string escaping. Practical payload class still exists under those constraints; for example an alias like `x'-alert`1`-'` yields a valid expression in the handler and invokes `alert` via tagged-template syntax before the built-in `requestSubmit()`. Report overstatement: `services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/organization/admin/resource/OrganizationResource.java` does call `RepresentationToModel.toModel()`, but actual mutation is blocked by `model/jpa/src/main/java/org/keycloak/organization/jpa/OrganizationAdapter.java:96-105`, which throws `ModelValidationException(\"Cannot change the alias\")` once the alias is already set. CSP does not save it: `server-spi-private/src/main/java/org/keycloak/models/BrowserSecurityHeaders.java:27` uses the default CSP from `ContentSecurityPolicyBuilder.create().build()`, and `server-spi-private/src/main/java/org/keycloak/models/ContentSecurityPolicyBuilder.java:31-36` shows that default is only `frame-src 'self'; frame-ancestors 'self'; object-src 'none';`; `testsuite/integration-arquillian/tests/base/src/test/java/org/keycloak/testsuite/forms/LoginTest.java:165-177` confirms login pages receive that default header.",
  "new_reason:": ""

  "report": "2
2. `CWE-79` stored XSS via organization alias rendered inside an inline `onclick` handler.
The organization selection page writes the organization alias into inline JavaScript at [select-organization.ftl](/data/lifengjie/LLM4Security/data/codex_java_projects/keycloak_codex/themes/src/main/resources/theme/base/login/select-organization.ftl:12). That page is reachable from the browser organization authenticator at [OrganizationAuthenticator.java](/data/lifengjie/LLM4Security/data/codex_java_projects/keycloak_codex/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/organization/authentication/authenticators/browser/OrganizationAuthenticator.java:262).
On the input side, organization creation only applies `ReservedCharValidator.validateNoSpace(...)` at [OrganizationsResource.java](/data/lifengjie/LLM4Security/data/codex_java_projects/keycloak_codex/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/organization/admin/resource/OrganizationsResource.java:107), and that validator does not reject quotes/backticks/pipes at [ReservedCharValidator.java](/data/lifengjie/LLM4Security/data/codex_java_projects/keycloak_codex/server-spi-private/src/main/java/org/keycloak/utils/ReservedCharValidator.java:35). Worse, organization update does not validate alias at all before copying it into the model at [OrganizationResource.java](/data/lifengjie/LLM4Security/data/codex_java_projects/keycloak_codex/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/organization/admin/resource/OrganizationResource.java:115) and [RepresentationToModel.java](/data/lifengjie/LLM4Security/data/codex_java_projects/keycloak_codex/server-spi-private/src/main/java/org/keycloak/models/utils/RepresentationToModel.java:1730).
A malicious alias can therefore break out of the inline handler and execute script when the victim clicks an organization entry on the selection page. This is another stored XSS path."
}
